author: nero_eth
content: are you saying there is a bug in the specs? Or is it more like dependable transactions in a test that allow you to test that your parallel execution optimization is working?

author: nero_eth
content: If it is the latter, there are a bunch of tests [here](https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/tree/forks/amsterdam/tests/benchmark/stateful/eip7928_block_level_access_lists) that should all have dependent transactions (where each tx depends on the prev ones), such that if you use the wrong pre-state for parallelization, you shouldn't be able to pass them.
